outbound_official.note: No single consolidated German government outbound visa-list page located. The Auswärtiges Amt (Federal Foreign Office, auswaertiges-amt.de) does not publish an analog to India's MEA vffin page listing destinations accessible to German ordinary-passport holders by visa category. Outbound entry requirements for German citizens are published per-destination within individual country travel advisories (Reise- und Sicherheitshinweise) under the 'Einreise und Zoll' section at https://www.auswaertiges-amt.de/de/reiseundsicherheit/reise-und-sicherheitshinweise. The main visa-requirements table at https://www.auswaertiges-amt.de/en/visa-service/231148-231148 covers INBOUND access to Germany (which foreign nationals are visa-free to enter Germany), not outbound German-citizen travel. Rely on destination-side data and per-country Reise- und Sicherheitshinweise pages.

name: Germany

visa_free.notes: All EU citizens enjoy freedom of movement under EU law; no visa or restriction on duration of stay as residents. For short stays up to 3 months only a valid identity card or passport is needed.

visa_free.nationality: EEA nationals (Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway) and Switzerland
visa_free.notes: Enjoy freedom of movement equivalent to EU citizens; can enter at any time with a valid identity card or passport, no visa required.

visa_policy.notes: Germany is a Schengen Area member. Visa-free nationals from non-EU/EEA countries may stay up to 90 days in any 180-day period. No visa-on-arrival or e-visa system exists; all visas must be obtained in advance at German diplomatic missions abroad. EU/EEA/Swiss nationals have unrestricted freedom of movement. Several nationalities (Australia, Canada, Israel, Japan, Korea, New Zealand, UK, USA) may also obtain residence permits after entry without a prior national visa. EES (Entry/Exit System) became fully operational on 10 April 2026. ETIAS (European Travel Information and Authorisation System) is expected to launch in Q4 2026; during a transitional period travelers without ETIAS will not be refused entry solely for that reason. Once mandatory, previously visa-free non-EU nationals will need ETIAS authorization for Schengen entry.

credential.subtype: any valid residence permit of a Schengen member state

conditional_access.conditions: Non-EU citizens holding a valid residence permit issued by any of the fully-applying Schengen states (Austria, Belgium, Bulgaria, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Greece, Hungary, Iceland, Italy, Latvia, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland) do not need a visa for visits to Germany of up to 90 days in any period of 180 days when not taking up work.

credential.subtype: Type C (short-stay) or Type D (national/long-stay) Schengen visa valid for Schengen states

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of a valid Schengen visa (text on the visa reads 'valid for Schengen states') can travel to Germany for up to 90 days in any period of 180 days. This is counted within the Schengen 90/180-day allowance and applies during the visa's validity period.

credential.subtype: residence card of a family member of a Union citizen (Directive 2004/38/EC)

conditional_access.conditions: Non-EU nationals who hold a valid 'residence card of a family member of a Union citizen' issued pursuant to EU Directive 2004/38/EC do not require a visa to enter Germany. Applies to spouses, registered partners, children, grandchildren under 21, and financially-dependent parents/grandparents of EU citizens exercising freedom of movement.

credential.subtype: any valid visa or national residence permit of EU/EEA/Schengen states

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of valid visas and national residence permits of EU and Schengen states (including EEA states) are exempt from the airport transit visa (Type A) requirement at German international airports (Frankfurt, Munich, Hamburg, Duesseldorf, Berlin-Brandenburg). This is an airside transit exemption only — travellers remain in the international transit area without entering Schengen territory.

Holders of a valid visa issued by the United States of America are exempt from the airport transit visa (Type A) requirement at German international airports (Frankfurt, Munich, Hamburg, Duesseldorf, Berlin-Brandenburg). This is an airside transit exemption only — travellers remain in the international transit area without entering Schengen territory.

Holders of a valid visa issued by Canada are exempt from the airport transit visa (Type A) requirement at German international airports (Frankfurt, Munich, Hamburg, Duesseldorf, Berlin-Brandenburg). This is an airside transit exemption only — travellers remain in the international transit area without entering Schengen territory.

Holders of a valid visa issued by Japan are exempt from the airport transit visa (Type A) requirement at German international airports (Frankfurt, Munich, Hamburg, Duesseldorf, Berlin-Brandenburg). This is an airside transit exemption only — travellers remain in the international transit area without entering Schengen territory.

credential.subtype: any valid US national residence permit

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of a valid national residence permit issued by the United States of America are exempt from the airport transit visa (Type A) requirement at German international airports (Frankfurt, Munich, Hamburg, Duesseldorf, Berlin-Brandenburg). This is an airside transit exemption only — travellers remain in the international transit area without entering Schengen territory.

credential.subtype: any valid Canadian national residence permit

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of a valid national residence permit issued by Canada are exempt from the airport transit visa (Type A) requirement at German international airports (Frankfurt, Munich, Hamburg, Duesseldorf, Berlin-Brandenburg). This is an airside transit exemption only — travellers remain in the international transit area without entering Schengen territory.

credential.subtype: any valid Japanese national residence permit

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of a valid national residence permit issued by Japan are exempt from the airport transit visa (Type A) requirement at German international airports (Frankfurt, Munich, Hamburg, Duesseldorf, Berlin-Brandenburg). This is an airside transit exemption only — travellers remain in the international transit area without entering Schengen territory.

credential.subtype: any valid Andorran national residence permit

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of a valid national residence permit issued by Andorra are exempt from the airport transit visa (Type A) requirement at German international airports (Frankfurt, Munich, Hamburg, Duesseldorf, Berlin-Brandenburg). This is an airside transit exemption only — travellers remain in the international transit area without entering Schengen territory.

credential.subtype: any valid San Marinese national residence permit

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of a valid national residence permit issued by San Marino are exempt from the airport transit visa (Type A) requirement at German international airports (Frankfurt, Munich, Hamburg, Duesseldorf, Berlin-Brandenburg). This is an airside transit exemption only — travellers remain in the international transit area without entering Schengen territory.

Per footnote 9 of the official German visa requirements table (auswaertiges-amt.de/en/visa-service/231148-231148): Georgian holders of official passports require a visa. This is an exception to the general Georgia ordinary-passport visa waiver. Official passports here means service/official government-issue passports; ordinary biometric passport holders are visa-free for up to 90 days.

cbi.notes: Germany does not have any citizenship-by-investment (CBI) program. Citizenship is obtained through naturalisation after meeting residency requirements (generally 5 years with the new Skilled Immigration Act for well-integrated individuals, or 8 years under standard rules), language proficiency, and integration criteria. Foreign nationals who naturalise must generally renounce prior citizenship, with exceptions for EU/Swiss nationals and certain other cases. Confirmed via Federal Foreign Office and BAMF official websites.

programs.name: Residence Permit for Self-Employment / Investors (Section 21 AufenthG)
programs.notes: Under Section 21(1) of the Residence Act, third-country nationals can obtain a residence permit for self-employment if there is an economic interest or regional demand for the business, a positive economic impact is expected, and financing is secured. No fixed minimum investment amount is specified in official sources — viability and economic impact are assessed case by case. Initial permit valid up to 3 years; settlement permit (permanent residence) possible after 3 years of successful operation. Persons over 45 must show adequate pension provision. For freelancers in liberal professions, Section 21(5) applies. Citizenship path generally 8 years (or 5 years under skilled immigration rules if applicable).

programs.name: EU Blue Card (Blaue Karte EU)
programs.eligibility: University degree holders or third-country nationals with specific professional experience; employment contract or binding job offer for at least 6 months; minimum gross annual salary of EUR 50,700 (general occupations) or EUR 45,934.20 for shortage occupations (STEM, IT, medicine, engineers, scientists, teachers, physicians, IT specialists with 3+ years experience). Shortage occupations have reduced salary thresholds.
programs.processing_time: Varies; initial residence permit issued for 4 years (or duration of contract plus 3 months if shorter)
programs.notes: Settlement permit (permanent residence) accessible after 27 months of employment with pension contributions; reduced to 21 months with B1-level German language proficiency. Spouses granted immediate, unlimited work permission without German language requirement for family reunification. Salary thresholds verified June 2026: EUR 50,700 general / EUR 45,934.20 shortage occupations.

programs.name: Fast-Track Procedure for Skilled Workers (Section 81a AufenthG)
programs.eligibility: Third-country skilled workers sponsored by a German employer. Employer initiates process at the local immigration authority (Auslaenderbehorde), signs cooperation agreement, and pays fee. Employer-held power of attorney enables faster processing of recognition, employment approval, and visa issuance.
programs.processing_time: Accelerated compared to standard procedure; minimum 4 weeks indicated for visa stage
programs.notes: Fee of EUR 411 charged to employer. Based on Section 81a of the Residence Act. Covers recognition of foreign professional qualifications, Federal Employment Agency approval, and visa issuance in an accelerated coordinated procedure. Source domain make-it-in-germany.com is the official German government portal for skilled immigration.

programs.name: Opportunity Card (Chancenkarte) — Job-Search Visa
programs.eligibility: Non-EU nationals who either (1) hold a foreign qualification recognised in Germany, or (2) score at least 6 points in the points system (based on qualifications, work experience, language skills, age, Germany ties). Must demonstrate sufficient financial resources (blocked bank account with min. EUR 1,091 net/month or declaration of commitment). German language knowledge at A1 level required.
programs.notes: Introduced under the new Skilled Immigration Act (Fachkraefteeinwanderungsgesetz). Allows entry to Germany to search for qualified employment without a pre-existing job offer. Initial duration for job search; if a concrete job offer is received but a regular work permit cannot yet be issued, an Opportunity Card extension (up to 2 years) is available providing access to qualified employment.

programs.name: Skilled Immigration Act (Fachkraefteeinwanderungsgesetz) — Skilled Worker Residence Permit
programs.eligibility: Third-country nationals with a recognized foreign vocational or academic qualification and a concrete employment offer. Expanded under 2023 reform to include experience-based skilled workers (without formal recognition in some cases) and a points-based pathway.
programs.processing_time: Standard procedure; fast-track option available via Section 81a
programs.notes: 2023 Skilled Immigration Act expanded access for skilled workers from non-EU countries. Path to settlement permit (permanent residence) after 4 years; reduced to 21-27 months for EU Blue Card holders. Citizenship path reduced to 5 years for particularly integrated individuals under the 2024 citizenship reform.

agreements: EU freedom of movement (Treaty on the Functioning of the European Union)
EEA freedom of movement (Agreement on the European Economic Area — Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway)

validation_notes: CORRECTION: Georgia conditional_access entry reformatted. Prior record had basis='passport_type' with passport_types=['diplomatic','service','official'] and level='visa_free', but the conditions text contradicted the level — it said holders REQUIRE a visa. This was logically inverted. Corrected to level='visa_required' with passport_types=['official'] matching footnote 9 wording from auswaertiges-amt.de.

CORRECTION: Footnote 5 (biometric passport requirement) added to notes for visa-free entries for Albania, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Georgia, Kosovo, Moldova, Montenegro, North Macedonia, Serbia, Seychelles, Ukraine per official table footnote citations. These were omitted in the prior record.

CORRECTION: EU Blue Card minimum salary thresholds added to fast_track entry: EUR 50,700 general / EUR 45,934.20 shortage occupations, sourced from make-it-in-germany.com glossary and search results. This gap is now closed.

CORRECTION: ETIAS/EES notes updated. EES (Entry/Exit System) became fully operational 10 April 2026, per European Commission official announcement. ETIAS expected Q4 2026 with transitional grace period. Prior notes said 'not yet in force at time of research' which is now outdated.
CORRECTION: Schengen residence permit conditional_access source URL updated from /faq/28-visa---eu-laender/606744 to /faq/28-visa-eu-laender-606744 to match confirmed active URL.

VERIFIED CORRECT: Jamaica and South Africa are NOT in the visa_free list — confirmed visa required per official auswaertiges-amt.de table. A prior intermediate scrape result incorrectly suggested they were visa-free; the definitive table confirms visa required.
VERIFIED CORRECT: Ukraine is correctly listed in visa_free (with biometric passport condition, footnote 5).
VERIFIED CORRECT: No visa-on-arrival, e-visa, or ETA category exists for Germany — all non-EU/EEA/CH nationals either have nationality-based visa waiver or must apply in advance.
gaps: Exact processing times for self-employment visa not stated on official pages.
ETIAS exact launch date within Q4 2026 not yet confirmed by European Commission — to be announced at least 6 months in advance per eu.europa.eu.

Diplomatic/service passport-specific bilateral visa waiver agreements for entry into Germany (beyond the Georgia footnote 9 restriction) not comprehensively listed in a single official public document.
OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholder status confers no special conditional entry privilege for Germany — no official German source found granting any credential-based waiver for OCI holders.

Airport transit visa exemption for holders of valid US/Canada/Japan visas confirmed official; however the official source does not specify whether 'valid' means 'previously used at least once' or 'not yet used'.
EU Blue Card salary thresholds are updated annually; figures of EUR 50,700 and EUR 45,934.20 reflect 2026 values per make-it-in-germany.com — should be re-verified annually.
